title: Space Tech Briefing — July 03, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts! It's Friday, July 03, 2026, and you're tuned into your daily dose of cosmic innovation and discovery. We've got some incredible stories for you today, so let's dive right in!

First up, a fascinating new development from the International Space Station! Our very own Sophie Adenot has been hard at work, conducting the ChlorISS experiment. She's been growing plants up there, uncovering a super-power that vegetation holds in space. Imagine plants not just for food, but actively "purifying" the air for our astronauts. It's a game-changer for long-duration missions, proving that even a little green can make a big difference in maintaining a healthy environment far from Earth. Truly groundbreaking research from the ISS!

And as we anticipate more incredible science, let's take a moment to look back at a truly breathtaking achievement that continues to astound us. Just last week, on June 24th, the European Space Agency unveiled something spectacular from the European Euclid telescope. We're talking about the *largest high-resolution photo ever taken in visible light* of the very heart of our Milky Way galaxy! This colossal 6-gigapixel mosaic reveals over 60 million stars in a single, stunning scene. Captured back in March 2025 during a 26-hour observation, Euclid precisely pointed its camera at the galactic bulge, the bright central region of the Milky Way, assembling nine distinct views – each covering an area larger than a full moon. To bring those incredible colors to life, the Euclid team ingeniously combined its visible light data with observations from the ground-based Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope. A truly unprecedented level of detail, showing us our galaxy's core like never before!

What a journey through the cosmos today! From orbital plant power to the stunning heart of our galaxy, space exploration never ceases to inspire. Thank you for joining us. Stay curious, keep looking up, and we'll be back tomorrow with more incredible space news. Have a fantastic day!
